topic.name: Oil and Gas Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and policies related to the management, taxation, and environmental impact of oil and gas extraction on the outer continental shelf.

Document 1: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act to declare that any royalty under an oil and gas lease on submerged lands shall apply to all oil removed and discharged into waters of the United States.
Increases the minimum royalty under such leases from 12.5% to 20% and requires the Secretary of the Interior to ensure fair return to the United States.

topic.name: Tax Credit Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on amending the Internal Revenue Code to provide various tax credits and reliefs related to child care and education.

Document 1: extends the tax rates for dividend and capital gain income
increased expensing allowance for depreciable business assets
Document 2: increase the rates and maximum allowable amount of the tax credit for employer-provided child care facilities
increase the eligibility threshold amount and rate of the household and dependent care tax credit
Document 3: increase the rates and maximum allowable amount of the tax credit for employer-provided child care facilities
increase the tax exclusion for employer-provided dependent care assistance

topic.name: Student Loan Forgiveness Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at modifying and enhancing student loan forgiveness programs for teachers and public service employees.

Document 1: modifies the qualifying service requirement of the teacher loan forgiveness program
permits a teacher who completes five full-time years of non-consecutive service to be eligible for loan forgiveness
Document 2: require the Secretary of Education to recalculate the cohort default rates for institutions of higher education
holds IHEs that participate in the DL program financially accountable for poor student outcomes
Document 3: direct the Department of Education to cancel 15%, 15%, 20%, 20%, and 30% of the amount a borrower owes after 2, 4, 6, 8, and 10 years of public service employment
requires borrowers availing themselves of such loan cancellation to submit an employment certification form

topic.name: Medicare and Medicaid Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the regulation and management of Medicare and Medicaid programs, focusing on drug affordability, physician self-referral, and enrollment processes.

Document 1: Medicare Prescription Drug Affordability Act of 2009
limit Medicare part D prescription drug plan cost increases
Document 2: Medicaid Physician Self-Referral Act of 2014
prohibit payment for a Medicaid designated health service furnished to an individual on the basis of a physician's referral
Document 3: revise the conditions for state use of an independent enrollment broker
ensure the independence of its enrollment activities from the interests of any managed care entity

topic.name: Tariff Amendments and Trade Policy
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative changes related to tariffs and duty treatments for specific goods under U.S. trade policy.

Document 1: Amends the Trade Act of 1974
authorize the President to designate certain comforter shells as eligible articles for duty-free treatment
Document 2: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
reduce temporarily the duty on lithium ion electrical storage batteries
Document 3: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
extend the temporary suspension of duty on leather basketballs

topic.name: Veterans' Benefits and Services
topic.description: Legislation focused on enhancing and streamlining benefits and services for veterans, including disability ratings, healthcare, and support for homeless veterans.

Document 1: Presumes a service connection for diagnosed hearing loss, tinnitus, or both, for veterans who...
Directs the Secretary of each military department to ensure that each member of the Armed Forces...
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs...to assign a pre-stabilization disability rating of: (1) 100%, for a veteran with a severe disability...
Terminates the pre-stabilization rating...
Document 3: This bill eliminates the minimum continuous active duty service requirement for homeless veterans to receive certain benefits...
Homeless veterans are exempted from disqualification for such benefits because of discharge or dismissal from the Armed Forces...

topic.name: Child Protection Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at preventing child abuse and exploitation through various legal measures and penalties.

Document 1: Prohibits the Secretary of Health and Human Services from making grants to a state for specified child abuse or neglect prevention and treatment programs until the state's laws provide for a criminal punishment.
subject to penalties for domestic assault by an habitual offender any person who commits three or more assaults against a child.
Document 2: Amends the Victims of Child Abuse Act of 1990 to specify the types of information that providers should include in reports of apparent child pornography violations.
authorize the National Center for Missing and Exploited Children to forward provider reports to appropriate state, local, and foreign law enforcement agencies.
Document 3: Amends the Telecommunications Act of 1934 to modify the prohibition against making obscene or harassing communications.

prohibit using the mail, any interactive computer service, telecommunications device, electronic communication service to intentionally disclose information of another individual with intent to facilitate specified criminal offenses involving sexual abuse.

topic.name: Financial Regulation Amendments
topic.description: Legislative changes aimed at modifying existing financial regulations and enhancing consumer protection in the banking and credit sectors.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act (FDIA) and the Federal Credit Union Act (FCUA) to redefine the standard maximum deposit insurance amount and the standard maximum share insurance amount as being $250,000, respectively.
Authorize the FDIC to impose special assessments upon depository institution holding companies to recover losses to the DIF.
Document 2: Amends the Truth In Lending Act to limit the annual percentage rate (APR) applicable to any consumer credit transaction to the maximum rate permitted by the laws of the state in which the consumer resides.
Document 3: Directs the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System to publish proposed revisions to the Small Bank Holding Company Policy Statement.
Amends the Truth in Lending Act (TILA) to require the Board to exempt from certain escrow or impound requirements a loan secured by a first lien on a consumer's principal dwelling.

topic.name: Land Transfer and Management Acts
topic.description: Legislation concerning the transfer and management of federal lands to local entities or tribes for specific uses.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to convey to Elko County...
Holds 373 acres of BLM administered land in trust for the Te-moak Tribe of Western Shoshone Indians of Nevada.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to convey to the local communities in South Dakota...
Requires the conveyed lands to continue to be used in the same manner and for the same purposes.
Document 3: Requires... the transfer of administrative jurisdiction over specified federal property... from the Secretary of the Army to the Secretary of the Interior...
Prohibits the Tribe from conducting gaming activities on land taken into trust pursuant to this Act.

topic.name: Political Campaign Transparency
topic.description: Legislation aimed at increasing transparency in political campaigns through financial disclosure requirements.

Document 1: require each candidate of a major party for the office of President to file with the Federal Election Commission (FEC) a copy of the candidate's income tax returns
the FEC shall request the Department of the Treasury to furnish the returns.
Document 2: require any candidate of a major party for the office of President to file with the Federal Election Commission (FEC) a copy of the candidate's income tax returns
the FEC to disclose to the public the applicable tax returns of any person who has been nominated as a candidate of a major party.

topic.name: Immigration Policy Amendments
topic.description: This cluster discusses legislative amendments related to the treatment and education of immigrants and unaccompanied alien children in the United States.

Document 1: UAC State Notification Act of 2014
require the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) or the Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS) to notify the governor of a state within 48 hours before the child's placement
Document 2: Strengthening America's Public Schools Through Promoting Foreign Investment Act
permit a lawful alien who enters the United States on a nonimmigrant student visa to attend a U.S. public secondary school for longer than one year
Document 3: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act
eliminate specified immigrant and labor certification petition filing deadlines for certain aliens physically present in the United States

topic.name: U.S. National Security Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on legislative measures related to U.S. national security, intelligence funding, and military support mechanisms.

Document 1: Directs the President to develop and transmit to the appropriate congressional committees, with intelligence support from the Director of National Intelligence, a comprehensive interagency strategy and implementation plan for long-term security and stability in Pakistan.
Authorizes appropriations for: (1) Pakistan; and (2) the Pakistan Counterinsurgency Capability Fund.
Document 2: Authorizes appropriations for FY2010 for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities of the following: (1) Office of the Director of National Intelligence (DNI); (2) Central Intelligence Agency (CIA); ...
Requires notification of the congressional intelligence committees in either case.
Document 3: Requires the Secretary of the Treasury, during a federal funding gap impacting the Armed Forces, to transfer to the Secretary of Defense ...
Prohibits any such transfer after December 31, 2011.

topic.name: Health and Food Safety Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on health, safety, and transparency in drug pricing and food labeling.

Document 1: require manufacturers of certain drugs and biological products to report to the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) price increases
HHS must publish manufacturer reports, a summary of those reports, and supporting analyses.
Document 2: require food that contains an ingredient from a genetically modified organism (GMO) to be labeled as genetically engineered
The labeling requirements of this bill preempt state and local labeling requirements.
Document 3: direct the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to distribute a summary notice containing product information about each food or food product subject to such recall
Requires that specified slaughterhouses, processing establishments, and grinding facilities perform specified tests for the presence of E. coli in ground beef.

topic.name: Mental Health Services Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at improving mental health services and support for various populations, including college students and individuals experiencing homelessness.

Document 1: Amends the Public Health Service Act to require the Secretary of Health and Human Services to award grants to improve mental and behavioral health services on college campuses.
Establishes the College Campus Task Force to discuss mental and behavioral health concerns.
Document 2: Amends the Public Health Service Act to revise and extend Projects for Assistance in Transition from Homelessness for individuals with serious mental illness.
Increases minimum program funding allotment for states to assist those at risk of homelessness.

topic.name: Agricultural Policy Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative amendments related to agricultural practices, commodity production, and regulations affecting farmers and agricultural transport.

Document 1: Amends the Farm Security and Rural Investment Act of 2002 to authorize the Secretary of Agriculture to consider national average market price variations for different classes of wheat
determining wheat producer eligibility for 2007 counter-cyclical payments.
Document 2: Amends the Food, Conservation, and Energy Act of 2008 to consider mung beans and pulse crops as covered commodities
producers on a farm may reduce the base acres for any covered commodity for a crop year by one acre for each acre used for the production of fruits or vegetables.
Document 3: Amends the Motor Carrier Safety Improvement Act of 1999 to revise exemptions from federal maximum driving and on-duty time motor carrier regulations
extends the exemptions to drivers transporting agricultural farm supplies.

topic.name: Iran Sanctions Legislation
topic.description: Legislation concerning financial sanctions and restrictions related to Iran's government and its activities, particularly in relation to international terrorism and nuclear proliferation.

Document 1: prohibits the use of funds by the United States to make a payment to the government of Iran
certifies to Congress that Iran has paid all compensatory damages awarded to a U.S. person
Document 2: directs the Secretary of the Treasury to report to Congress on efforts to ensure that the Society for Worldwide Interbank Financial Telecommunication has terminated services to Iranian financial institutions
authorizes the President to impose specified sanctions on an entity that has not terminated any such relationship
Document 3: declares the sense of Congress about sanctions for the government of Iran's illicit nuclear activities
requires cancellation of any lease whose lessee has failed to make such a disclosure or makes a false disclosure

topic.name: Veterans and Disability Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to benefits and support for veterans, servicemembers, and individuals with disabilities.

Document 1: Timley Due Process for the Disabled Act of 2008
ensure timely disability determinations
Document 2: Servicemember Retirement Improvement Act
maximum allowable contribution to Thrift Savings Plans
Document 3: Veterans TRICARE Choice Act
eligible to participate in the TRICARE program

topic.name: Infrastructure Development and Management
topic.description: Legislation focused on enhancing and managing various infrastructure systems in the United States.

Document 1: Establishes an independent National Infrastructure Bank
designate qualified transit, public housing, water, highway, bridge, aviation, freight, passenger rail, or road infrastructure projects
Document 2: Designates the Northeast Corridor as a high-speed rail corridor
eligible for grants for high-speed rail capital projects
Document 3: Directs the Administrator of the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) to develop a plan for opening up to commercial flights
Requires the FAA Administrator to establish an Aviation Traveler Task Force

topic.name: Coral Reef and Fisheries Management
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at the conservation and sustainable management of coral reef ecosystems and fisheries in the Gulf of Mexico.

Document 1: Coral Reef Sustainability Through Innovation Act of 2016
promote coral reef research and conservation
Document 2: State Fisheries Act of 2014
managing fisheries in the Gulf of Mexico
Document 3: Gulf of Mexico Red Snapper Fishery Protection Act
conduct a benchmark stock assessment of the Gulf of Mexico red snapper fishery

topic.name: Labor and Marriage Rights
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to labor rights, union membership, and marriage equality issues, particularly focusing on employment discrimination based on sexual orientation and gender identity.

Document 1: Amends the National Labor Relations Act and the Railway Labor Act to repeal those provisions that permit employers... to require employees to join a union as a condition of employment.
Document 2: Prohibits the government of the District of Columbia from issuing a marriage license to any couple of the same sex until the people of the District have the opportunity to hold a referendum...
Document 3: Prohibits employment discrimination on the basis of actual or perceived sexual orientation or gender identity by covered entities.

topic.name: Telecommunications Regulation
topic.description: The documents discuss regulations related to telecommunications services, including number portability and Internet poker licensing.

Document 1: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to declare that a provider of voice services has the duty to provide number portability
Requires designation of 9-1-1 as the universal emergency telephone number within the United States for both wireline and wireless telephone service.
Document 2: Requires that the costs of establishing numbering administration arrangements and number portability be borne by all voice service providers
Requires designation of 9-1-1 as the universal emergency telephone number within the United States for both wireline and wireless telephone service.
Document 3: Prohibits a person from operating an Internet poker facility without a license in good standing
Establishes a five-year term for initial licenses, subject to renewal and transfer requirements.

topic.name: Cybersecurity and Privacy Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses laws aimed at enhancing cybersecurity measures, protecting personal information, and regulating the responsibilities of service providers in relation to data breaches and theft.

Document 1: prohibit a provider of commercial mobile or commercial mobile data service from providing service on a mobile electronic device reported to such provider as stolen
capability of deleting, from a remote location, all information that was placed on such a device after its manufacture.
Document 2: authority of the Comptroller General to audit and evaluate financial transactions, programs, and activities of elements of the intelligence community
safeguards to protect the unauthorized disclosure of all classified and other sensitive information
Document 3: criminal penalties for intentional failures to provide required notices of a security breach involving sensitive personally identifiable information

Requires a person who owns or possesses data in electronic form containing a means of identification and who has knowledge of a major security breach of the system containing such data maintained by such person to provide prompt notice

topic.name: Environmental Policy and Management
topic.description: The documents focus on legislation related to the management and restoration of natural resources and ecosystems in the United States.

Document 1: ecosystem restoration projects on National Forest System land
restore terrestrial habitat; sustain water quality, water flows, or watershed health
Document 2: annual deposit into the BLM Wind Energy and Solar Energy Permit Processing Improvement Fund
processing of wind energy and solar energy permit applications

topic.name: Small Business Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at supporting and enhancing small business operations in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Small Business Act to suspend fees under the 7(a) program
Authorizes the SBA to guarantee up to 90% of a loan made by a private lender to a small business
Document 2: Amends the Small Business Act to direct the Administrator of the SBA to assign an SBA employee to coordinate SBA programs
Requires reports from the coordinator to the congressional small business committees on coordinator activities
Document 3: Amends the Congressional Budget and Impoundment Control Act of 1974 to require the Director of the CBO to include estimates of potential job creation or job loss
Requires each agency to place on its website its plan for the periodic review of rules

topic.name: Designations of Public Facilities
topic.description: The documents discuss the designation and conveyance of specific public facilities and lands for community use.

Document 1: Federal Land Conveyance Act of 2013
convey to the Amy Biehl High School Foundation certain federal lands
Document 2: Designates the Department of Veterans Affairs community-based outpatient clinic
Loren R. Kaufman Memorial Veterans' Clinic
Document 3: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service
John J. Shivnen Post Office Building

topic.name: Election Administration and Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the administration of elections, including the regulation of voting systems and the treatment of territories in election processes.

Document 1: Amends the Sex Offender Notification and Registration Act to require jurisdictions to prohibit registered sex offenders from accepting a position of employment...

Authorize the Attorney General to award grants and enter into contracts with public agencies or nonprofit private organizations to establish a community accessible sex offender tips and support program.
Document 2: Directs the Election Assistance Commission to make a single payment to the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands...
Directs the Commonwealth to use such payment to carry out the same election administration improvement activities.
Document 3: Amends the Help America Vote Act of 2002 to prohibit a voting system used in a state in a federal election from containing or using any election-dedicated voting system technology...
Requires a state or local election official... to hold it in escrow; and disclose information regarding it to a qualified person.

topic.name: U.S. Legislation on Cuba and Detention
topic.description: This cluster covers U.S. legislative actions related to trade and diplomatic relations with Cuba, as well as regulations concerning the detention of individuals at Guantanamo Bay.

Document 1: Amends the Tariff Act of 1930 to revise eligibility requirements for the drawback (refund) of duties on merchandise exported from the United States.
Document 2: Amends the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961 to repeal the embargo placed upon all trade with Cuba.

Permits: (1) installation and maintenance of telecommunications equipment and facilities in Cuba, including telecommunications services between the United States and Cuba; and (2) travel to and from Cuba by U.S. citizens or residents.
Document 3: Prohibits the use of funds to transfer, release, or assist in the transfer or release to or within the United States, its territories, or possessions of Khalid Sheikh Mohammed or any other detainee who is not a U.S. citizen or a member of the Armed Forces.

topic.name: U.S. Legislative Acts on Social Issues
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various U.S. legislative acts addressing social issues, including marriage definitions, healthcare for specific populations, and support for aging individuals.

Document 1: Prohibits the term 'marriage' from including any relationship that the state... does not recognize as a marriage
the term 'spouse' from including an individual who is a party to a relationship that is not recognized as a marriage
Document 2: Amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act to provide full reimbursement for medical assistance given to a Native Hawaiian
through a federally-qualified health center or a Native Hawaiian health care system
Document 3: Amends the Older Americans Act of 1965 to reauthorize through FY2018 appropriations
grants for state and community programs on aging

topic.name: Abortion Regulations and Consent
topic.description: This topic encompasses legal frameworks and requirements surrounding abortion procedures, parental involvement, and informed consent in the United States.

Document 1: Prohibits the Secretary of State from contributing to any organization or program that supports or participates in the management of a program of coercive abortion or involuntary sterilization.

Defines 'supports or participates in' as providing material, financial, or technical support to any government, agency, or nongovernmental organization that enforces or implements a program that includes coercive abortion or involuntary sterilization.
Document 2: Amends the federal criminal code to prohibit transporting a minor child across a state line to obtain an abortion.
Makes an exception for an abortion necessary to save the life of the minor.
Document 3: Requires abortion providers to perform an obstetric ultrasound on the pregnant woman before she gives informed consent to any part of an abortion.
Provides for civil penalties for willful failure to comply.

topic.name: Postal Service Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the operations, benefits, and environmental initiatives of the United States Postal Service.

Document 1: Requires a mail-order pharmacy to inform a customer to whom it dispenses a prescription brand name drug when a generic drug that is the pharmaceutical equivalent becomes available.
Permits a mail-order pharmacy to substitute a generic drug for a brand name drug.
Document 2: Amends the National Energy Conservation Policy Act to expand the definition of 'energy or water conservation measure' under such Act to include, in the case of a contract in which the U.S. Postal Service (USPS) is a party.
Directs the Postmaster General to develop guidelines for USPS vehicles that provide for specified carbon dioxide emissions and fuel economy standards.
Document 3: Prescribes the 'average pay' and the appropriate percentage thereof to be used in determining annuities for civilian employment with the United States Postal Service (USPS).
Expresses the intent of Congress that this Act shall apply to allocations of past, present, and future benefit liabilities between the USPS and the Treasury.

topic.name: Lung Cancer Mortality Reduction
topic.description: Legislative initiatives aimed at reducing lung cancer mortality rates through research, early detection, and targeted programs.

Document 1: Lung Cancer Mortality Reduction Program to achieve a reduction of at least 25% in the mortality rate of lung cancer by 2015.
establish an early disease research and management program targeted at the high incidence and mortality rates of lung cancer among minority and low income populations.
Document 3: comprehensive program to achieve a 50% reduction in the mortality rate of lung cancer by 2020.
provision of funds to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) to establish a Lung Cancer Early Detection Program.

topic.name: Veterans and First Responders Honors
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to honoring veterans and first responders through ceremonial practices and federal program evaluations.

Document 1: Prohibits the Secretary of Veterans Affairs from authorizing an honor guard to participate in the funeral of any veteran...
Expresses the sense of Congress that 13-fold flag recitations are a historical tradition...
Document 2: Permits the flag of the United States to be flown at half-staff in the event of the death of a first responder...

topic.name: Regulatory Safety and Compliance
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at establishing safety standards and compliance requirements across various sectors including agriculture, gas storage, and drone operations.

Document 1: Amends the Consolidated Farm and Rural Development Act to expand eligibility for loans.
Deems specified entities eligible for farm ownership and operating loans.
Document 2: Directs the Department of Transportation (DOT) to prescribe minimum safety standards for underground gas storage facilities.
A state authority may adopt additional or more stringent safety standards for intrastate underground gas storage facilities.
Document 3: Amends the federal criminal code to make it a crime to operate an unmanned aircraft that disrupts the operation of an aircraft.
A violator is subject to a fine, a prison term, or both.

topic.name: Unemployment and Compensation Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving unemployment compensation systems and providing financial assistance to affected individuals and communities.

Document 1: amends the Middle Class Tax Relief and Job Creation Act of 2012
extends federal financing of the programs for an additional two years
Document 2: amends Title III of the Social Security Act
authorize the Secretary of Labor to enter into agreements with applicant states

topic.name: Legislative Changes in Federal Law
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts that modify federal laws and judicial structures in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the federal judicial code to divide the Ninth Judicial Circuit
Requires the President to appoint five additional circuit judges for the new Ninth Circuit.
Document 2: Authorizes TransCanada Keystone Pipeline, L.P. to construct, connect, operate, and maintain pipeline facilities
Declares that no Executive Order, provision of law, or presidential permit shall be required for such facilities.
Document 3: Repeals the USA PATRIOT Act and the FISA Amendments Act of 2008
Prohibits information relating to a U.S. person from being acquired pursuant to FISA without a valid warrant based on probable cause.

topic.name: Veterans Affairs and Military Support
topic.description: Legislation focused on the support, healthcare, and recognition of military personnel and veterans in the United States.

Document 1: Congress should not take any action that will endanger U.S. military forces in the field
such action would undermine their safety or harm their effectiveness
Document 2: Extends the period of eligibility for hospital care, medical services, or nursing home care for veterans
who served in a theater of combat operations during a period of war after the Persian Gulf War
Document 3: Deems service of a person as a member of the United States Cadet Nurse Corps to be active military service
Requires the Secretary of Defense to issue an honorable discharge to each person whose qualified service warrants one

topic.name: Regulatory Changes in Consumer Products
topic.description: This cluster discusses legislative measures aimed at regulating consumer products, including labeling requirements and safety standards.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Energy to study and report to Congress with recommendations for appropriate methods and standards for requiring motor vehicle fuel suppliers to disclose country-of-origin information.
Prohibits such regulations from requiring the listing of more than one country-of-origin for a fuel blend.
Document 2: Amends the Consumer Product Safety Act to allow a secondhand seller to sell, offer for sale, or distribute a product or substance that is not in conformity with a consumer product safety rule relating to lead in children's products.

Prohibits either the Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) or any state attorney general from initiating an enforcement proceeding under the Consumer Product Safety Act regarding product certification and labeling.
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow a tax credit up to $5,000 annually for the qualified tuition and related expenses of an individual taxpayer.
Allows such credit for four years of undergraduate education expenses and six years of graduate or professional education expenses.

topic.name: Renewable Energy Development
topic.description: The documents focus on legislative efforts to promote and manage renewable energy resources and related research initiatives.

Document 1: Requires NOAA to develop a plan for a comprehensive and integrated ocean, coastal, Great Lakes, and atmosphere science program to support renewable energy development.
Establishes a renewable energy information library and data portal to function as a cross-agency repository of data pertinent to renewable energy development.
Document 2: Extends specified energy programs under the Food, Conservation, and Energy Act of 2008.

topic.name: Public Resource Management
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at the management and improvement of public resources, including parks, nutrition programs, and marine sanctuaries.

Document 1: conduct a special resources study to determine the suitability and feasibility of entering into public-private partnerships
operate East Potomac Golf Course, Langston Golf Course, and Rock Creek Park Golf Course
Document 2: amends the Richard B. Russell National School Act to allow an Indian tribe to assume responsibility for administration of the school breakfast program
increase the amount of administrative funds paid to an Indian tribe
Document 3: U.S. policy to protect and preserve living and other resources of the Gulf of the Farallones National Marine Sanctuary
prohibits the issuance of a lease or permit authorizing mineral or hydrocarbon exploration

topic.name: Government Policy Amendments
topic.description: This cluster discusses various amendments to federal laws concerning defense, firearms, and agricultural policies.

Document 1: Amends the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2013
ensure that each beneficiary other than an active-duty beneficiary who is enrolled in TRICARE Prime
Document 2: Denying Firearms and Explosives to Dangerous Terrorists Act of 2007
grants the Attorney General the authority to deny the transfer of firearms
Document 3: Amends the Farm Security and Rural Investment Act of 2002
require the Secretary of Agriculture to permanently reduce base acres

topic.name: Public Health and Social Policy
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at improving public health, social welfare, and community assistance.

Document 1: federal funds to establish or carry out a program of distributing sterile syringes
reduce the transmission of bloodborne pathogens, including HIV and viral hepatitis
Document 2: support the sustainable recovery and rebuilding of Haiti
promote development and rebuilding led by all levels of Haitian government
Document 3: require a national criminal background check for employees of child care providers
search of the national Sex Offender Registry

topic.name: Regulatory Oversight and Financial Accountability
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing regulatory oversight and ensuring financial accountability within federal agencies and financial institutions.

Document 1: Requires federal agency guidance documents to be submitted for congressional review prior to implementation.
Defines 'guidance document' and 'major guidance document' with significant economic implications.
Document 2: Requires federal agencies to report on charges for services and recommendations for revising those charges.
Mandates evaluation of equitable user payment and public benefit from agency programs.
Document 3: Amends the Bank Holding Company Act regarding prohibitions on proprietary trading.
Addresses the investment restrictions for banking entities under certain conditions.

topic.name: Legislative Actions and Policies
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at addressing specific social issues, including suicide prevention for military members, land trust for indigenous tribes, and communication regulations.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Defense (DOD) to ensure that each of the following individuals receive, at least once every 90 days, a telephone call from properly-trained DOD personnel to determine their emotional, psychological, medical, and career needs and concerns
Requires annual reports from the Secretary to Congress on the number of members so referred, as well as their health and career status
Document 2: Holds all right, title, and interest of the United States in and to approximately 10 acres of federal land, shown as parcel A on a specified map, in trust for the Pascua Yaqui Tribe of Arizona
Prohibits gaming on such lands
Document 3: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to prohibit the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) from having the authority to require broadcasters to present opposing viewpoints on controversial issues of public importance
Commonly referred to as the Fairness Doctrine.

topic.name: U.S. Federal Legislation on Economic Policy
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative measures aimed at regulating economic policies, including monetary policy, tax regulations, and support for individuals with disabilities.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Reserve Act (FRA) to direct the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System to promote the goal of long-term price stability.
Directs the Board and the FOMC to make such information available to the public on a website.
Document 2: Prohibits the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) from implementing or enforcing any requirement of the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act (PPACA).
Requires such certification to be submitted to Congress.
Document 3: This bill amends the Internal Revenue Code, with respect to qualified ABLE programs, to increase the threshold for eligibility for such programs from age 26 to age 46.

topic.name: Government Accountability and Advocacy
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts aimed at enhancing government oversight, protecting individual rights, and improving support systems for vulnerable populations.

Document 1: Establishes as an independent establishment in the executive branch an Office of the Special Advocate to protect individual rights
Requires the Attorney General to publicly disclose all decisions issued by the FISA Court
Document 2: Authorizes the President to provide assistance to Georgia for humanitarian needs and governance
Enhance Russian- and Georgian-language Internet and broadcast capacity for the Voice of America
Document 3: Directs each military department Secretary to implement a program providing a Special Victims' Counsel
Requires the Director to also collect, maintain, and disseminate information related to sexual assault prevention and response programs

topic.name: Energy Efficiency and Mitigation
topic.description: This topic encompasses initiatives and regulations aimed at improving energy efficiency in various sectors while addressing environmental impacts such as noise and greenhouse gas emissions.

Document 1: passenger facility fee imposed at a large hub airport to carry out noise mitigation
buildings are adversely affected by airport noise
Document 2: expanding industrial energy efficiency
supporting state activities involving both facilities and vehicle fleets
Document 3: enhancing the availability of private financing for clean energy technology development
increase energy efficiency and generate electricity from renewable energy resources

topic.name: Mental Health and Support Programs
topic.description: Legislation focused on mental health interventions and support systems for vulnerable populations.

Document 1: incorporate preventive and early-intervention measures
reduce the likelihood that personnel in combat will develop post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD)
Document 2: support the establishment or expansion and operation of programs using networks of public and private community entities to provide mentoring for children in foster care
provide for the discharge or cancellation of the federal student loan indebtedness of an eligible mentor

topic.name: Environmental Legislation and Policy
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts aimed at environmental protection, natural resource management, and the rights of indigenous communities.

Document 1: Reauthorize the program of hydroelectric production incentives
improving efficiency
Document 2: exclude funds paid by Minnesota to members of the Grand Portage Band
restrict tribal rights to hunt and fish
Document 3: establish a Great Lakes basin initiative for agricultural non-point source pollution prevention
Great Lakes State defined as Illinois, Indiana, Michigan, Minnesota, New York, Ohio, Pennsylvania, or Wisconsin

topic.name: Children's Welfare Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the protection, funding, and oversight of programs for children in the United States.

Document 1: Truth in Regulations Act of 2016 requires public comment on significant guidance documents affecting children and families.
The bill mandates transparency in agency procedures that impact children's welfare.
Document 2: Children's Budget Act requires detailed analysis of appropriations for children's programs.
The act emphasizes accountability in federal budgeting for child-related initiatives.
Document 3: Violence Against Children Act of 2009 provides federal assistance for the investigation of crimes against children.
The act establishes guidelines for law enforcement and support services for child victims.

topic.name: Health and Economic Development Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on legislation aimed at addressing health care needs and economic development in specific regions affected by historical events.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of State to provide assistance to address the health care needs of covered individuals.
Requires such assistance to include assistance to institutions in Vietnam that provide health care to such individuals.
Document 2: Requires the Authority to develop plans and programs for the economic development of the Southwest border region.
Authorizes the Authority to approve grants for regional infrastructure development and improvement.
